key_insight: Gravitational instabilities can be understood as the progressive evolution of a hidden system state, where damage accumulation, stress redistribution, and external forcing jointly drive the system toward failure.
engineering_relevance: The framework gives engineers and hazard analysts a unified way to connect physical mechanisms, monitoring data, and forecasting models for earlier and more robust instability assessment.
method: Model instability as a latent state that evolves over time
Link internal damage and stress changes with external forcing
Infer proximity to failure from incomplete, indirect observations
evidence_signal: The abstract is conceptual rather than data-driven, so it argues for a common framework across hazards instead of reporting a specific experimental or validation dataset.
watch_item: Its usefulness depends on how well the latent state can be inferred from sparse observations, which may be difficult in real field settings.
paper_title: A Unified Conceptual Framework for Gravitational Instabilities: From Latent System State to Failure
